2604.06135.title: Shot-Based Quantum Encoding: A Data-Loading Paradigm for Quantum Neural Networks
2604.06135.paper_summary: Efficient data loading remains a bottleneck for near-term quantum machine-learning. Existing schemes (angle, amplitude, and basis encoding) either underuse the exponential Hilbert-space capacity or require circuit depths that exceed the coherence budgets of noisy intermediate-scale quantum hardware. We introduce Shot-Based Quantum Encoding (SBQE), a data embedding strategy that distributes the hardware's native resource, shots, according to a data-dependent classical distribution over multiple initial quantum states. By treating the shot counts as a learnable degree of freedom, SBQE produces a mixed-state representation whose expectation values are linear in the classical probabilities and can therefore be composed with non-linear activation functions. We show that SBQE is structurally equivalent to a multilayer perceptron whose weights are realised by quantum circuits, and we describe a hardware-compatible implementation protocol. Benchmarks on Fashion MNIST and Semeion handwritten digits, with ten independent initialisations per model, show that SBQE achieves 89.1% +/- 0.9% test accuracy on Semeion (reducing error by 5.3% relative to amplitude encoding and matching a width-matched classical network) and 80.95% +/- 0.10% on Fashion MNIST (exceeding amplitude encoding by +2.0% and a linear multilayer perceptron by +1.3%), all without any data-encoding gates.
2604.06135.paper_summary_zh: 有效的數據加載仍然是近期量子機器學習的瓶頸。現有的方案（角度編碼、幅度編碼和基礎編碼）要麼未充分利用指數希爾伯特空間的容量，要麼需要超過噪聲中等規模量子硬體的相干預算的電路深度。我們介紹了基於拍攝的量子編碼（SBQE），這是一種數據嵌入策略，根據多個初始量子狀態上的數據依賴性經典分佈來分配硬體的原生資源——拍攝。通過將拍攝數量視為可學習的自由度，SBQE 生成一種混合態表示，其期望值在經典概率中是線性的，因此可以與非線性激活函數組合。我們表明，SBQE 在結構上等同於一個多層感知器，其權重由量子電路實現，並且我們描述了一個與硬體兼容的實施協議。在 Fashion MNIST 和 Semeion 手寫數字上的基準測試中，每個模型有十次獨立初始化，顯示 SBQE 在 Semeion 上達到 89.1% +/- 0.9% 的測試準確率（相對於幅度編碼減少 5.3% 的誤差，並與寬度匹配的經典網絡相匹配），在 Fashion MNIST 上達到 80.95% +/- 0.10%（超過幅度編碼 +2.0% 和線性多層感知器 +1.3%），所有這些都不需要任何數據編碼閘。
2604.06135.author: Basil Kyriacou et.al.
2604.06135.authors: Basil Kyriacou, Viktoria Patapovich, Maniraman Periyasamy, Alexey Melnikov
